2606.07172.title: Textual Supervision Enhances Geospatial Representations in Vision-Language Models
2606.07172.paper_summary: Geospatial understanding is a critical yet underexplored dimension in the development of machine learning systems for tasks such as image geolocation and spatial reasoning. In this work, we analyze the geospatial representations acquired by three model families: vision-only architectures (e.g., ViT), vision-language models (e.g., CLIP), and large-scale multimodal foundation models (e.g., LLaVA, Qwen, and Gemma). By evaluating across image clusters, including people, landmarks, and everyday objects, grouped based on the degree of localizability, we reveal systematic gaps in spatial accuracy and show that textual supervision enhances the learning of geospatial representations. Our findings suggest the role of language as an effective complementary modality for encoding spatial context and multimodal learning as a key direction for advancing geospatial AI.
2606.07172.paper_summary_zh: 地理空間理解是機器學習系統在影像地理定位和空間推理等任務發展中一個關鍵但尚未充分探索的維度。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們分析了三種模型家族所獲得的地理空間表徵：僅視覺架構（例如，ViT）、視覺-語言模型（例如，CLIP）以及大規模多模態基礎模型（例如，LLaVA、Qwen 和 Gemma）。<lb/>通過在影像集群中進行評估，包括人、地標和日常物品，根據可定位性程度進行分組，我們揭示了空間準確性中的系統性差距，並顯示文本監督增強了地理空間表徵的學習。<lb/>我們的研究結果表明，語言作為編碼空間上下文的有效補充模態的角色，以及多模態學習作為推進地理空間人工智慧的關鍵方向。
2606.07172.author: Marcelo Sartori Locatelli et.al.
2606.07172.authors: Marcelo Sartori Locatelli, Fernando Tonucci, Jea Kwon, Luiz Felipe Vecchietti, Bryan Nathanael Wijaya, Cheng Yaw Low, Virgilio Almeida, Meeyoung Cha
